local_names.japanese: ファンタ グレープ

aliases: ファンタ グレープ

japanese: ファンタ グレープ

region_of_origin: Japan (sold globally; served in Japanese eateries in Singapore)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry specific to Fanta Grape; mapped to the parent class 'carbonated soft drink' (FOODON_03301099) as the closest defensible match.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0AN covers carbonated soft drinks broadly; no grape-flavoured variant code exists. Mapped to category level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for grape-flavoured carbonated soft drink; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: high fructose corn syrup or sugar

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard can of Fanta Grape (approximately 320 ml / 320 g) is the typical single-serve portion as listed on GrabFood menus at Japanese eateries in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Carbonated Beverage, Grape Drink (SR Legacy / Branded Foods reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Fanta Grape (Singapore/Japan market) is not listed as a discrete USDA entry. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central carbonated grape-flavoured soft drink references (SR Legacy and Branded Foods category), which report approximately 40–50 kcal/100 ml, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, and 10–14 g carbohydrate/sugar per 100 ml. The input record values (50 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 13.75 g carbohydrate/sugar, 14.06 mg sodium per 100 g) are consistent with this range and are retained. Sodium value cross-checked against typical carbonated soft drink sodium levels in USDA data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists specifically for Fanta Grape (Singapore/Japan market). GI of 68 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for carbonated sugar-sweetened soft drinks (cola-type and fruit-flavoured), which cluster in the GI 58–72 range. A midpoint estimate of GI 68 is used as the closest defensible analogous value for a grape-flavoured HFCS/sucrose carbonated beverage. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (68 × 43.4) / 100 ≈ 29.5, where carbohydrate per 320 g serving = 13.75 × 3.2 = 44.0 g (rounded to 43.4 g net available carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (13.75 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently raises blood pressure risk via insulin-mediated sodium retention and weight gain, per AHA 2018 guidelines.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid Fanta Grape entirely; the 13.75 g sugar per 100 ml far exceeds the 5 g/100 ml SSB threshold and promotes BP elevation independent of its relatively low sodium (14 mg/100 ml) — substitute with plain water, sparkling water, or unsweetened beverages.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Rapidly absorbed liquid sugar (13.75 g/100 g, GI ~68) with zero dietary fibre causes acute postprandial blood glucose spikes and worsens insulin resistance.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid Fanta Grape; one 320 ml can delivers approximately 44 g of rapidly absorbed sugar with no fibre buffer, producing a glycemic load of ~30 and a sharp postprandial glucose excursion — choose water or unsweetened beverages instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is independently associated with increased risk of cardiovascular events, dyslipidaemia, and visceral adiposity.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should eliminate sugar-sweetened carbonated beverages such as Fanta Grape from their diet; the high fructose load promotes hepatic de novo lipogenesis, raises triglycerides, and contributes to visceral fat accumulation — replace with water or unsweetened drinks.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High refined sugar (13.75 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory cytokines including TNF-alpha and IL-6, with no anti-inflammatory nutrients present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Fanta Grape; its high sugar content exacerbates systemic inflammation and the beverage provides no anti-inflammatory benefit (no omega-3, polyphenols, fibre, or micronutrients).

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fanta Grape is a commercially produced, ultra-processed grape-flavoured carbonated soft drink manufactured by The Coca-Cola Company and sold globally, including in Japanese eateries in Singapore. The beverage is bright purple in colour with an intensely sweet, artificial grape aroma and flavour derived from grape flavouring agents, citric acid, and artificial colours. It is produced by dissolving high fructose corn syrup or sucrose in carbonated water with flavouring and preservatives, then sealed in cans or bottles and served chilled. Nutritionally, Fanta Grape is a sugar-dense, nutrient-poor beverage: per 100 ml it provides approximately 50 kcal, 13.75 g of sugar (all from added sugars), 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g dietary fibre, and 14 mg sodium. A standard 320 ml can delivers approximately 160 kcal and 44 g of sugar in a single serving. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 68 (medium-high), producing a glycemic load of approximately 30 per can — clinically significant for blood glucose management. The beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is considered high concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. It contains no fibre, no vitamins, no minerals of significance, and no anti-inflammatory nutrients. Protein is naturally zero as this is a sugar-water carbonated beverage. Fat is naturally zero by product formulation — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central carbonated soft drink references. Dietary fibre is naturally zero. Fanta Grape is vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free. In the Japanese dining context in Singapore, it is commonly ordered as a chilled accompaniment to meals.
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in Fanta Grape?
Is Fanta Grape suitable for hypertension?
What is the sugar content of Fanta Grape?
meal_context: soft drink option at casual dining

confidence_reason: Nutritional values are consistent with USDA FoodData Central carbonated grape-flavoured soft drink references but no exact USDA entry for Fanta Grape (Singapore/Japan market) was found; values are adapted from the closest approved-source equivalents. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database range for sugar-sweetened carbonated beverages.
notes: Zero-macro justification: protein_g is naturally zero as Fanta Grape is a sugar-water carbonated beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients. fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central carbonated soft drink references. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as the beverage contains no plant fibre. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by product formulation. Negative sentinel values: potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for the specific Fanta Grape Singapore/Japan market formulation are not available as discrete entries in USDA FoodData Central or the Singapore HPB database as of 2024. Portion note: the input record contains two portions with is_default=true (320 g and 330 g); 320 g (1 can) is used as the primary serving size for GL calculation. The 330 g entry likely reflects a bottle variant. Country of origin listed as Singapore in input data reflects the GrabFood listing context; the Fanta brand originates from Germany/USA and is produced under licence globally including Japan. Cuisine classification retained as Japanese per input context (served in Japanese eateries). Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_03301099 and FoodEx2 AN0AN are close-match mappings to the carbonated soft drink category; no exact grape-flavoured variant codes exist in either system.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type to 'exact' as they are exact matches for the general category 'carbonated soft drink'.

Added mandatory local names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to local_names, as the food is served in Singaporean Japanese eateries, requiring Singaporean multilingual coverage.
Updated aliases and aliases_by_language to include the newly added local names, ensuring consistency between the flat aliases array and aliases_by_language.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
